spec_match.rationale: Visualises the five `AudioContextState` values â€” `suspended`, `running`, `interrupted`, `closed`, plus the initial state â€” and the legal transitions between them. That is precisely the new shape the spec adds for v136.

interactive.rationale: Seven listeners on transition buttons drive a live state-machine diagram; the active node highlights as the user invokes each method on a real AudioContext.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises `the full set of AudioContextState transitions on one screen ... Buttons drive each legal call, the diagram lights up the active node`. Page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Strong coverage of state transitions including the irreversible `closed` terminal (8 edge-term hits in source). The illegal-transition rejection paths are part of what the diagram exposes.

references.rationale: `see also` now cites `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5172068166139904` and the Web Audio API `AudioContextState` spec section.

design_system.rationale: 35 CSS variable references, no raw hex, no button override mismatches.

openQuestions.title: Add a feature-detect probe
openQuestions.detail: Unlike the sibling concept pages, this one lacks the `typeof AudioContext... interrupted in AudioContextState` probe. Add one at the top so the user can see whether their browser actually exposes the new state.

openQuestions.title: Surface illegal-transition error messages
openQuestions.detail: When the user presses a button corresponding to an illegal transition (e.g. `resume()` from `closed`), capture the resulting promise rejection and show it in-page rather than failing silently â€” that is the most pedagogically useful part of a state-machine teach.

openQuestions.title: Animate the active node transition
openQuestions.detail: A short CSS transition or visible arrow that animates the move between nodes would make the diagram easier to follow when transitions chain (e.g. `running â†’ interrupted â†’ running` from a single button press).

summary: Educational state-machine view of the full AudioContextState surface with the required ChromeStatus/Web Audio references now present. A feature-detect probe and illegal-transition feedback would lift it from visualisation to teach.
